descriptiveIncome.definition: Median after-tax income of economic families and unattached individuals
descriptiveIncome.limitations: This is an overall descriptive reference. It is not matched to one household size, tenure, or geography-specific Market Basket Measure threshold and does not determine the national verdict.

mbm-poverty-rate.label: Share below the Market Basket Measure
mbm-poverty-rate.definition: Percentage of people below Canada's official poverty line under the 2023-base Market Basket Measure
mbm-poverty-rate.limitations: Latest-vintage revised survey estimate; sampling and non-sampling error apply.

deep-poverty-rate.label: Share in deep poverty
deep-poverty-rate.definition: Percentage with income below 75% of Canada's official poverty line
deep-poverty-rate.source: Statistics Canada, Dimensions of Poverty Hub, 2024
deep-poverty-rate.limitations: This is an income-threshold measure, not a direct household budget.

food-insecurity-rate.label: Household food-insecurity exposure
food-insecurity-rate.definition: Percentage of people living in households reporting marginal, moderate, or severe food insecurity
food-insecurity-rate.limitations: The measure captures household food-access experience and is not a food-price index.

shelter-burden-30-rate.label: Households spending at least 30% of income on shelter
shelter-burden-30-rate.definition: Share of households in the provinces spending 30% or more of income on shelter costs
shelter-burden-30-rate.limitations: Older than the income and food-security evidence; provincial household coverage and tenure differences apply.

shelter-burden-50-rate.label: Households spending at least 50% of income on shelter
shelter-burden-50-rate.limitations: The official category is defined, but a verified current national estimate has not yet been integrated.

core-housing-need-rate.label: Households in core housing need
core-housing-need-rate.definition: Share living in unsuitable, inadequate, or unaffordable housing and unable to afford acceptable local alternatives
core-housing-need-rate.limitations: Census-period measure; substantially older than current price and income observations.

representativeBudgets.householdType: Toronto Market Basket Measure reference family of four
representativeBudgets.incomeDefinition: Median after-tax income of Canadian two-parent families with children
representativeBudgets.limitations: Illustrative only. National family income is not matched to the Toronto MBM family by geography and exact composition, so this case cannot determine a national verdict.

matchedHouseholdCases.missing: Matching household-size and geography-specific MBM basket
Matching household-size and geography-specific MBM basket
Matching single-person and geography-specific MBM basket
Matching senior household and geography-specific basic-needs basket

affordability.unresolvedReason: Matched household-type income and geography-specific basic-needs baskets are incomplete, so the national absolute verdict is unresolved.

us.referencePopulation: People and households in the United States

descriptiveIncome.source: U.S. Census Bureau, Income in the United States: 2024
descriptiveIncome.limitations: Descriptive only; it is not household-type matched and does not determine the national verdict.

official-poverty-rate.label: Official poverty rate
official-poverty-rate.definition: Share below the official U.S. poverty threshold
official-poverty-rate.source: U.S. Census Bureau, Income in the United States: 2024
official-poverty-rate.limitations: Official thresholds vary by family size and composition but not geography.

deep-poverty-rate.label: Share below 50% of the poverty threshold
deep-poverty-rate.source: U.S. Census Bureau, Income in the United States: 2024
deep-poverty-rate.limitations: Income threshold measure, not a direct household budget.

food-insecurity-rate.label: Food-insecure households
food-insecurity-rate.definition: Share of households experiencing food insecurity
food-insecurity-rate.source: USDA Economic Research Service, Household Food Security in the United States in 2024
food-insecurity-rate.limitations: Household experiential measure; not a retail food-price index.

very-low-food-security-rate.label: Households with very low food security
very-low-food-security-rate.definition: Share of households with disrupted eating patterns and reduced food intake
very-low-food-security-rate.limitations: Survey estimate subject to sampling and non-sampling error.

housing-burden-30-rate.label: Households with housing-cost burden
housing-burden-30-rate.definition: Share spending more than 30% of household income on housing costs
housing-burden-30-rate.source: U.S. Census Bureau, ACS Housing Availability and Affordability: 2023
housing-burden-30-rate.limitations: All-household national estimate; renter and owner burdens differ substantially.

housing-burden-50-rate.label: Households with severe housing-cost burden
housing-burden-50-rate.definition: Share spending more than 50% of household income on housing costs
housing-burden-50-rate.source: U.S. Census Bureau, ACS Housing Availability and Affordability: 2023
housing-burden-50-rate.limitations: All-household national estimate; renter and owner burdens differ substantially.

representativeBudgets.source: U.S. Census Bureau and BLS Consumer Expenditure Surveys
representativeBudgets.limitations: Illustrative only. Median Census households and average BLS consumer units are not matched, so this case cannot determine a national verdict.

affordability.unresolvedReason: Matched household-type income and official basic-needs cases are incomplete, so the national absolute verdict is unresolved.

global.unsupportedReason: No valid globally comparable absolute household-income and essential-cost distribution is available in the project.
